results.reason: The script requires a credentials.json config file at a hardcoded path (~/.openclaw/agents/gzh-assistant/wechat/credentials.json) and also requires a cover image file to exist before it will reach any network call beyond token retrieval. It reads no credentials from environment variables. Additionally, reaching the draft submission network call requires a real cover image upload to succeed first, making a clean offline invocation of the full flow impossible without file-system setup that goes beyond simple env vars.

The backup action requires actual local files to exist at /root/.openclaw/workspace/ to collect anything meaningful, and the restore action requires interactive confirmation input. The script calls GitHub's API via the PyGithub library with complex multi-step operations (get_repo, get_git_ref, create_git_blob, create_git_tree, create_git_commit, ref.edit) rather than a single documented HTTP endpoint with simple query parameters. There is no single documented request endpoint with simple argv-driven query/body parameters that can be meaningfully intercepted and compared between argv_a and argv_b.

plan.py accepts no CLI arguments whatsoever—it is a zero-argument command that always calls RegisterArkClawCombo with an empty body. There is no user-supplied value to vary between argv_a and argv_b, so the two-invocation requirement cannot be satisfied.
The entry script requires --captions to be an existing local JSON file (os.path.isfile check exits with code -1 if missing). A network call cannot be reached without a real input file on disk.
constructor JSON parse: Extra data: line 17 column 1 (char 432)

The script does not make a network call itself; it only prints a JavaScript snippet to stdout meant to be eval'd in a browser context via 'eval "$(python scripts/search-ads.py ...)"'. There is no HTTP request built or executed by the Python script, so there is nothing to intercept at the Python/network level.

cloud_manager.py does not make a direct HTTP API request to a documented remote endpoint. It looks up assets in local CSV files and downloads from URLs stored in those CSVs. The network call is a file download to a URL from a local database (not a documented API), and without real CSV data files containing valid URLs, no network call will be reached. There is no documented API host/path to target.

The auto_trader.py script does not accept command-line arguments for API keys or query parameters that would vary the network request in a testable way. It reads config from ~/.openclaw/auto_trader_config.json and ~/.openclaw/config.yaml, requires sibling skill imports (kalshi-command-center), and its network calls (Kalshi API, Anthropic API, Slack webhook) are driven entirely by the config file and internal pipeline logic — not by CLI arguments. There is no --query or similar argv that would produce two distinct, diffable network requests. Additionally, it imports from a sibling skill directory (kalshi-command-center/scripts) that is not present, making it impossible to reach a network call without a real file/service setup.
constructor JSON parse: Extra data: line 18 column 1 (char 402)

The entry script _xml2jsonl.py is a stdin-to-stdout XML converter utility, not a network-calling CLI. It reads XML from stdin and writes JSONL to stdout — it makes no HTTP requests itself. The actual network calls are made by the main kipris-cli entry point (not provided), which pipes its XML response through this helper. Running this script alone cannot reach any network endpoint.

The entry script is an interactive OAuth flow that opens a browser and waits for a callback redirect. It has no CLI arguments and cannot be driven to a network call without real user interaction in a browser.

The script requires a --keypair argument pointing to a valid Solana keypair JSON file on disk. It reads and parses this file before making any network call, so no network request can be reached without a real input file.

The hook reads all input from stdin (a JSON blob with tool_name/tool_input), not from argv. There are no CLI arguments to vary; the network call cannot be driven or differentiated via argv_a/argv_b.
